title: Drepanophyllum lineare
intro: Drepanophyllum lineare is a plant species commonly known as "Rangamati's feather fern" or "Linear-leaved drepanophyllum," which is a type of fern native to India. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Drepanophyllum lineare include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, this plant is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and it is also used in the form of a powder or a tincture to make various herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, and ointments.
taxonomy: Drepanophyllum lineare is classified within the plant family Schizaeaceae, which is a group of climbing ferns. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics, such as its distinctive leaf shape and climbing habit. Within the Schizaeaceae family, Drepanophyllum lineare is further classified within the genus Drepanophyllum. The species is also classified as a member of the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. Overall, Drepanophyllum lineare is classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida.
